data.title: Buy Sunteck Realty; target of Rs 520: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Sunteck Realty

Sunteck Reality (SRIN) reported healthy pre-sales growth of 22% YoY, along with strong 39% YoY collection growth in Q4. SRIN’s proven ability to market ultra-luxury projects, aggressive and multi-pronged land acquisition capabilities in various micro markets across Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) is an interesting play on Mumbai’s high value real estate market. We expect the company’s pre-sales to grow to 25% CAGR over FY26-28E, supported by launch acceleration, including the planned Dubai JV project in 2HFY27.

Outlook

Further given likely strong cash flow generation, we see SRIN to step up new project additions which will be a key catalyst for stock performance. Maintain ‘Buy’ rating with revised TP of Rs520share.

Disclaimer

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: ADANIENSOL: FY26 revenue and EBITDA grew strongly, with record capex and expanding smart metering portfolio
data.description:

data.title: Sweden's Oxe Marine Q1 revenue drops 66%, losses widen amid project delays
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden diesel outboard maker's Q1 revenue fell sharply yr/yr amid project delays

  • Q1 EPS and EBITDA losses widened as demand softened and new business was limited

  • Company raised SEK 60 mln in new equity financing to strengthen balance sheet

Outlook

  • OXE Marine says short-term trading environment remains challenging due to project delays and tariffs

  • Company notes increase in enquiries going into Q2

  • OXE Marine expects opportunities from new sales team additions will take time to convert

Result Drivers

  • PROJECT DELAYS - Co said delays in key governmental projects, especially in the US, led to lower sales and overall financial performance

  • SOFTER DEMAND - Co attributed weaker results to a slower marine environment, tariff-driven price increases in the US, and conflict in the Middle East affecting logistics and tourism segments

  • COST CONTROL - Co said it maintained cost control and kept gross margin steady despite lower revenue

Company press release:

data.title: Switzerland's BB Biotech Q1 net loss narrows amid portfolio rotation
data.description:
Overview

  • Switzerland biotech investor's Q1 net loss narrowed sharply year-over-year

  • Company's share price outperformed Nasdaq Biotechnology Index and discount to NAV narrowed

  • Portfolio expanded to 30 holdings, investment guidelines broadened to 20–50 positions

Outlook

  • Company says macroeconomic uncertainty and shifting geopolitics continue to influence equity markets

  • BB Biotech expects several portfolio holdings to reach clinical and regulatory milestones in 2026

  • Company is integrating AI-enabled analytics to enhance investment process and risk assessment

Result Drivers

  • PORTFOLIO REPOSITIONING - Co said active portfolio rotation, including eleven new investments and increased large-cap exposure, reshaped holdings and contributed to results

  • M&A IMPACT - Acquisition of Terns Pharmaceuticals by Merck contributed positively to quarterly performance

  • CLINICAL UPDATES - Some holdings saw negative share price reactions after clinical updates came in slightly below market expectations

data.title: Canada's Cohere and Germany's Aleph Alpha to announce merger, Handelsblatt reports
data.description:
Artificial intelligence companies Cohere of Canada and Aleph Alpha of Germany have agreed to merge, newspaper Handelsblatt reported on Friday.

Citing government and industry sources, the paper said the combined company was intended as an alternative to U.S. technology companies, providing digital public-sector services to businesses and public authorities.

The deal is to be announced at 11 a.m. (0900 GMT) in Berlin, with Digital Minister Karsten Wildberger and his Canadian counterpart Evan Solomon in attendance, the report said.

data.title: Sweden's Nordnet Q1 profit hits record on trading activity, new customers
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden-based digital investment platform's Q1 operating profit and income reached record highs

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 rose to SEK 3.32 from SEK 3.13 yr/yr

  • Net savings and new customer additions among highest since 2020/2021, driven by strong trading

Outlook

  • Nordnet expects full-year costs to be in line with target of about 8% annual increase

  • Company says focus remains on implementing AI for internal processes and customer offerings

Result Drivers

  • NET SAVINGS AND CUSTOMER GROWTH - Co said record net savings and 77,500 new customers were key drivers of quarterly results, per CEO Rasmus Järborg

  • TRADING ACTIVITY AND FUND SAVINGS - Strong growth in brokerage and funds attributed to high trading activity and continued high fund savings

  • NET INTEREST INCOME - Turnaround in net interest income due to rising market interest rates and increased deposits

data.title: Norway's Tomra Systems Q1 revenue rises, but drop in margins drags EPS
data.description:
Overview

  • Norway resource optimization firm's Q1 revenue rose 9% yr/yr to 334 mln EUR

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 fell to 0.02 EUR from 0.03 EUR

  • Recycling segment revenue declined 19% amid continued weak order intake and challenging market

Outlook

  • Company says Recycling continues to face challenging market conditions with low order intake

  • Tomra expects cost reduction program in Recycling to gradually improve profitability

  • Company sees positive market sentiment continuing in Food segment

Result Drivers

  • COLLECTION GROWTH - Revenue growth in Collection driven by steady performance in existing markets and new installations in Poland and Portugal

  • RECYCLING DECLINE - Recycling revenue fell due to recent low order intake and a challenging market environment

  • PRODUCT MIX IMPACT - Gross margin negatively affected by short-term product mix in all segments and low volumes in Recycling
